layout.path: Layout & Grid / Using sm for padding and md for vertical/horizontal spacing in the same component is valid. It is rare/not ideal to mix different semantic sizes for the same token type (e.g., pad-x-sm + pad-y-md).
layout.name: Using sm for padding and md for vertical/horizontal spacing in the same component is valid. It is rare/not ideal to mix different semantic sizes for the same token type (e.g., pad-x-sm + pad-y-md).

layout.path: Layout & Grid / Form <-> Specific Use Cases Gap XS Token: This token is reserved for tight spacing where elements are closely related. Its primary use is for the space between a form label and its input field (e.g., a text field or dropdown menu). / Ellipse
layout.name: Ellipse

layout.path: Layout & Grid / Core Spacing Principles for Elements The primary spacing tokens are tied to the typographic hierarchy. Use the following as your default guide: Headline Text: Use element-gap-lg and element-x/y-lg Title Text: Use element-gap-md and element-x/y-md Body Text: Use element-gap-smand element-x/y-sm However, mixing tokens is still allowed (see mixing Tokens) if absolutely necessary
layout.name: Core Spacing Principles for Elements The primary spacing tokens are tied to the typographic hierarchy. Use the following as your default guide: Headline Text: Use element-gap-lg and element-x/y-lg Title Text: Use element-gap-md and element-x/y-md Body Text: Use element-gap-smand element-x/y-sm However, mixing tokens is still allowed (see mixing Tokens) if absolutely necessary
